title: United Nations
html:
"You are free to examine the Magi's data recorders. There is no record of any such incident."
 This article or section does not cite any (or nearly enough) references or sources.
Please discuss this issue on the talk page and/or begin editing the page.

"The person in charge exists to take responsibility."
 This article/section is poorly organized, making the content a lot less accessible and user-friendly than it ought to be. Take any measures necessary to correct this.
Please discuss this issue, or begin editing the page.

"Making something... Nurturing something is really great. You can see and learn so many things from the process."
Please help improve this article or section by expanding it.
If you have questions on what to improve/expand, please discuss on the talk page.

The United Nations features in Neon Genesis Evangelion but its powers and functioning appear to be significantly different and expanded from its real-life counterpart. Many of the details regarding the functioning of the UN within the universe of Neon Genesis Evangelion are not made explicit, and must be extrapolated from supplemental sources such as the Classified Information in addition to those inferred within the show itself.

Nerv ostensibly functions as a special agency under the UN. The Human Instrumentality Committee, although formed of high-ranking Seele members, is ostensibly a small council within the United Nations. Although it is evident on numerous occasions that Seele has great influence within the UN, the full extent and nature of the relationship between both organizations is never made explicit.

Unlike in the real world, the UN in Evangelion appears to hold direct control over military forces. The fleet seen in Episode 08 is referred to as though it was under direct UN control rather than, as in real life, being a collection of vessels from the individual member nations' navies.

A newspaper from 2002, seen in Episode 21, announces "UN moves headquarters to Japan in 5 years: Candidate City is Tokyo-2." Thus, it would seem that due to the chaos caused by Second Impact, UN headquarters was moved from New York City to Tokyo-2. [Source needed]

Organizations
Seele | Gehirn | United Nations | IPEA | JSSDF | Wille | Kredit
Special Agency Nerv:
Nerv Headquarters | Nerv 1st Branch | Nerv 2nd Branch | Nerv 3rd Branch
Nerv 7th Branch (Tabgha Base) | Bethany Base | Golgotha Base

Many of the details regarding the functioning of the UN within the universe of Neon Genesis Evangelion are not made explicit, and must be extrapolated from supplemental sources such as the Classified Information in addition to those inferred within the show itself.

chunks.content:
Article: United Nations
The United Nations features in Neon Genesis Evangelion but its powers and functioning appear to be significantly different and expanded from its real-life counterpart. Many of the details regarding the functioning of the UN within the universe of Neon Genesis Evangelion are not made explicit, and must be extrapolated from supplemental sources such as the Classified Information in addition to those inferred within the show itself.

Nerv ostensibly functions as a special agency under the UN. The Human Instrumentality Committee, although formed of high-ranking Seele members, is ostensibly a small council within the United Nations. Although it is evident on numerous occasions that Seele has great influence within the UN, the full extent and nature of the relationship between both organizations is never made explicit. 

Unlike in the real world, the UN in Evangelion appears to hold direct control over military forces. The fleet seen in Episode 08 is referred to as though it was under direct UN control rather than, as in real life, being a collection of vessels from the individual member nations' navies.

A newspaper from 2002, seen in Episode 21, announces "UN moves headquarters to Japan in 5 years:  Candidate City is Tokyo-2."  Thus, it would seem that due to the chaos caused by Second Impact, UN headquarters was moved from New York City to Tokyo-2.
